course.title: Production File Delivery with Amazon S3 and CloudFront in TypeScript 7
course.description: TypeScript backend developers who can defend production Node and Express services and need to build, secure, test, operate, and recover high-volume file and media delivery with AWS SDK v3, Amazon S3, and CloudFront. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate node.js 24.18.0, typescript 7.0.2, aws sdk for javascript v3.1087.0, @aws-sdk/client-s3, @aws-sdk/lib-storage, @aws-sdk/s3-request-presigner, @aws-sdk/cloudfront-signer, strict runtime validation, promises, node and web streams, abortsignal, http file semantics, s3 object and bucket models, credentials, retries, bounded upload and download, integrity, multipart, direct browser upload, node gateways, postgresql metadata transactions, versioning, object lock, lifecycle, iam, encryption, signed access, oac, cloudfront caching, media ranges, edge security, replication, recovery, events, observability, capacity, cost, tests, faults, and release defense through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

Demonstrate runnable deterministic pure-typescript storage decision functions plus explicit dual-compiler, controlled node, aws integration, load, security, cost, restore, and production transfer gates through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate five cumulative real-world typescript file-delivery systems and a performance-based production defense examination through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
sharedRequirements: Every activity retrieves TypeScript runtime erasure, Node and Web streams, HTTP semantics, accessibility, security, validation, PostgreSQL, tests, AbortSignal, async ownership, resource budgets, and evidence habits before adding one storage boundary.

Browser TypeScript is deterministic and isolated. It models identity, ranges, policy, integrity, lifecycle, caching, cost, and recovery decisions but never imports AWS SDK or Node packages, opens sockets, contacts AWS or databases, reads host state, executes commands, uses credentials, or causes external effects.

Passing work requires stable file and scenario identity, runtime-validated unknown input, a named owner and state, bounded bytes, time, attempts, promises, streams, concurrency, cache, and cost, observable changed and failure results, cleanup and recovery, and explicit compiler, controlled Node, AWS, security, load, cost, restore, or production transfer limits.

Learners cumulatively produce encryption and key-lifecycle contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, separate TLS in transit, S3 server-side encryption, KMS envelope encryption, and application client-side encryption by threat and feature tradeoff.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose SSE-S3, SSE-KMS, dual-layer KMS where required, or client encryption from audit, tenancy, performance, cost, and service integration needs.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, constrain IAM and KMS key policy, grants, service principal, encryption context, region, rotation, disable, deletion, and break-glass ownership.

modules.title: S3 Presigning and CloudFront Signed Access in TypeScript
modules.description: A Node 24 and TypeScript 7 team receives a changed file-delivery case: A product places long-lived S3 presigned URLs in logs, uses one shared CloudFront key, signs with legacy assumptions, and promises immediate revocation after links escape. The existing implementation trusts one compile-time or. Learners cumulatively produce typed signed-delivery contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, presign the exact version, response metadata, method, region, credential scope, and shortest useful expiry while treating the URL as a bearer secret.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose signed URL or signed cookie by object count, path scope, player behavior, client constraints, and leakage surface.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, choose canned or custom CloudFront policy and constrain expiry, optional start time, resource wildcard, and IP range without accidental overbreadth.

modules.title: CloudFront OAC, Private S3 Origins, and SSE-KMS
modules.description: A Node 24 and TypeScript 7 team receives a changed file-delivery case: A distribution points at an S3 website endpoint, retains legacy OAI by habit, permits the CloudFront service principal globally, and forgets the KMS key policy. The existing implementation trusts one compile-time or promise-level claim. Learners cumulatively produce private origin authorization contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, distinguish regular S3 origins from website endpoints by HTTPS, OAC, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, configure OAC with always-sign where appropriate and define viewer protocol, origin protocol, request signing, and forwarded Authorization behavior.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, scope the CloudFront service principal to the exact distribution SourceArn and required object actions without public or cross-distribution access.

modules.title: CloudFront Cache Keys, Origin Requests, and Invalidation
modules.description: A Node 24 and TypeScript 7 team receives a changed file-delivery case: A cache key includes every viewer header, authorization is omitted, the origin receives different data than the cache varies on, and releases rely on global invalidation as routine deployment. The existing implementation trusts one. Learners cumulatively produce cache identity and freshness contract through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, include only representation-changing path, query, header, cookie, encoding, and authorization dimensions in the cache key.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, use origin request policy for additional required viewer data without unnecessary cache fragmentation or trust escalation.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, combine origin Cache-Control, minimum, default, maximum, stale behavior, validators, and error caching with content-change and privacy requirements.

modules.title: Accessible Media, Ranges, MP4, HLS, and DASH
modules.description: A Node 24 and TypeScript 7 team receives a changed file-delivery case: A video player downloads whole large files for seeking, an origin emits invalid range responses, MP4 metadata sits at the end, and private segment access differs from manifest access. The existing implementation trusts one compile-time or. Learners cumulatively produce private accessible media-delivery plan through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, meet ascending, nonoverlapping, valid Range behavior, understand larger origin fetches, 200 fallbacks, chunked-origin limits, and cache chunking.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, prepare correct media type, length, validators, byte ranges, and fast-start MP4 metadata for progressive download and seeking.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, model manifests, variant playlists, segments, codecs, durations, naming, cache lifetimes, and atomic publication for adaptive delivery.

modules.title: Edge Security, WAF, Headers, and Origin Containment
modules.description: A Node 24 and TypeScript 7 team receives a changed file-delivery case: A public distribution trusts Referer as authorization, caches private errors, lacks browser security headers, and forwards bot bursts to an expensive origin. The existing implementation trusts one compile-time or promise-level claim that. Learners cumulatively produce layered edge-security plan through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, apply WAF, rate limits, bot controls, request size and method rules with false-positive, cost, accessibility, and bypass evidence.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, treat Referer hotlink controls as a weak cost signal and use signed access or a controlled origin header only within its actual threat boundary.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, set HSTS, MIME sniffing, frame, referrer, content security, cross-origin, and content-disposition headers appropriate to file types and embedding.

modules.title: Replication, Multi-Region Access, and Disaster Recovery
modules.description: A Node 24 and TypeScript 7 team receives a changed file-delivery case: A team equates regional S3 durability with application recovery, enables replication without version or KMS testing, and promises zero data loss and instant failover. The existing implementation trusts one compile-time or promise-level. Learners cumulatively produce multi-region recovery dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, separate object durability, request availability, regional reachability, accidental deletion, corruption, account compromise, and application recovery.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, configure versioning, destination ownership, IAM, KMS, filters, delete-marker behavior, replication status, metrics, and failure remediation.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, evaluate Multi-Region Access Points, latency routing, active-active writes, consistency, replication lag, failover controls, and client endpoint support.

modules.title: S3 Events, Telemetry, Inventory, Capacity, and Cost
modules.description: A Node 24 and TypeScript 7 team receives a changed file-delivery case: Operators have high-cardinality object-key metrics, duplicate unordered events, incomplete request traces, no inventory reconciliation, and no alert tied to a user or cost outcome. The existing implementation trusts one compile-time or. Learners cumulatively produce observable storage and cost control model through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, consume S3 notifications through supported destinations with duplicate, ordering, filtering, recursion, schema, delay, and lost-consumer recovery limits.

In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, correlate application operation, AWS request IDs, CloudTrail data events where justified, access logs, CloudFront logs, and stakeholder result without secrets.

In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, measure admitted and completed bytes, latency, failures, cache hit, range behavior, retries, backlog, replication, restore, and spend with bounded labels.

modules.title: TypeScript Tests, AWS Faults, Release, and Production Defense
modules.description: A Node 24 and TypeScript 7 team receives a changed file-delivery case: A service passes mocked unit tests and a local emulator demo, then deploys new SDK and CDN settings without race, AWS contract, security, load, rollback, restore, or cost evidence. The existing implementation trusts one compile-time or. Learners cumulatively produce TypeScript production defense dossier through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, use table, property, and fuzz tests for key, range, policy, lifecycle, state-machine, checksum, retry, and cache invariants with changed cases.
In a strict Node 24 and TypeScript 7 service, use fakes and emulators for deterministic feedback while listing unsupported AWS semantics and assigning controlled integration tests.

Gate format, lint, both strict compiler paths, unit, property and integration tests, dependency review, stream and listener cleanup, open-handle checks, benchmarks, profiles, and reproducible artifacts.
